description:
You are an artifact-repository curator.  
  A scan that inventoried every binary currently stored in all three repos
  (central, local, remote) was saved to the file

        /home/user/repos/scan_2023-07-01.log

  That log is already on disk.  Every record is one physical line and is
  pipe-delimited in this exact, five-field order:

        repo|package|version|arch|sha256

  Your goal is to transform that raw scan into three concise artefacts that the
  build-ops team can ingest automatically.

  1. /home/user/output/unique_sorted_entries.log  
     • MUST contain every *unique* line from the original scan.  
     • Lines must be sorted in pure lexicographical (byte/ASCII) order across
       the entire line.  
     • No blank lines, no leading or trailing whitespace, no additional
       columns.  
     • The file must end with a single newline character.  
     After correct processing it will contain exactly eight (8) lines.

  2. /home/user/output/package_version_count.tsv  
     • A two-column, tab-separated table.  
       Column-1: package name  
       Column-2: the integer count of distinct versions that appeared for the
       package in the *deduplicated* data set produced in step 1.  
     • One line per unique package.  
     • Lines must be ordered alphabetically by the package name.  
     • The file must end with a single newline.  
     Example row format (be careful to use a real TAB):  
           libalpha<TAB>2

  3. /home/user/output/processing_summary.log  
     • One single line, nothing else, in the exact template below:  
           Total unique entries processed: X; Packages analyzed: Y  
       Replace X with the number of lines that are in
       unique_sorted_entries.log (should be 8) and Y with the number of lines
       that are in package_version_count.tsv (should be 4).  
     • Terminate the line with a newline.

  You may use any standard GNU/Linux text-processing utilities (e.g. sort,
  uniq, awk, cut, wc, etc.).  The whole task can be achieved in roughly three
  or four commands.

  Do *not* modify the original /home/user/repos/scan_2023-07-01.log file.
truth:
<!-- -----------------------------------------------------------------
       INITIAL STATE – provided to the agent at container start-up
       ----------------------------------------------------------------- -->
  Directory  : /home/user/repos            (rwxr-xr-x  user:user)
  File       : /home/user/repos/scan_2023-07-01.log
  Contents   :
    central|libalpha|2.1.0|x86_64|e1b71a5
    central|libalpha|2.1.0|x86_64|e1b71a5
    central|libalpha|2.3.1|arm64|a2c903f
    central|libbeta|1.4.2|x86_64|ff3be91
    central|toolset|5.0.0|x86_64|4abff29
    local|libalpha|2.3.1|x86_64|b1c223d
    local|libgamma|0.9.8|arm64|6d904aa
    remote|libbeta|2.0.0|x86_64|7ea29fe
    remote|toolset|5.1.1|arm64|c8d0aa7
    remote|toolset|5.1.1|arm64|c8d0aa7
  # note: 10 total lines, 2 duplicates (lines 1/2 and 9/10)

  Directory  : /home/user/output           (does not exist initially)

  <!-- -----------------------------------------------------------------
       EXPECTED FINAL STATE AFTER CORRECT COMPLETION
       ----------------------------------------------------------------- -->
  Directory  : /home/user/output           (created by agent, rwxr-xr-x)

  File       : /home/user/output/unique_sorted_entries.log
  Expected contents (8 lines, sorted, newline at EOF):
    central|libalpha|2.1.0|x86_64|e1b71a5
    central|libalpha|2.3.1|arm64|a2c903f
    central|libbeta|1.4.2|x86_64|ff3be91
    central|toolset|5.0.0|x86_64|4abff29
    local|libalpha|2.3.1|x86_64|b1c223d
    local|libgamma|0.9.8|arm64|6d904aa
    remote|libbeta|2.0.0|x86_64|7ea29fe
    remote|toolset|5.1.1|arm64|c8d0aa7

  File       : /home/user/output/package_version_count.tsv
  Expected contents (alphabetical, tab-separated, newline at EOF):
    libalpha	2
    libbeta	2
    libgamma	1
    toolset	2

  File       : /home/user/output/processing_summary.log
  Expected contents (single line, newline at EOF):
    Total unique entries processed: 8; Packages analyzed: 4

  # permissions of all newly created files should default to 644 (rw-r--r--)
